own_review.issue: Plugin 'de.thetaphi.forbiddenapis' is declared in the plugins block WITHOUT 'apply false', unlike spotbugs/scoverage/shadow (lines 42-44), even though it is explicitly applied per-subproject at line 801. It therefore gets applied to the root project (harmless no-op since root has no java plugin, but inconsistent with the established pattern for subproject-only quality plugins).

ignoreFailures = true makes the check report-only. Intentional per the PR description; the human reviewer confirms that without it nearly every module fails. Should carry a comment/JIRA reference and a follow-up plan to flip to false.

Only forbiddenApisMain is wired into test.dependsOn; forbiddenApisTest is not. This actually matches the spotbugs convention (line 805 wires only spotbugsMain; line 860 disables spotbugsTest via taskGraph) but deviates from checkstyle (both Main and Test). Note the plugin already hooks the umbrella 'forbiddenApis' task onto 'check' by default, so test-sourceset scanning still happens on './gradlew check'; the explicit dependsOn mainly affects bare 'test' invocations. Worth a clarifying comment either way.
The diff removes the trailing newline at end of file ('\ No newline at end of file'); the base file ends with a newline (verified via hexdump). Trivial style regression / future diff noise.

:core sets java srcDirs=[] and joint-compiles its 19 Java files through scalac. Because forbidden-apis is a bytecode (class-file) scanner and the Gradle plugin checks sourceSet.output.classesDirs, :core's Java classes ARE still covered via the scala output dir — but scalac-generated bytecode for the Scala sources is scanned too, a known source of false positives that often leads projects to exclude Scala modules. Informational nuance, masked for now by ignoreFailures=true.

bundledSignatures 'jdk-unsafe' without version suffix resolves against the task's targetCompatibility, which defaults to the running JVM (the build sets options.release=11 but not targetCompatibility). Building on a JDK newer than the signatures bundled in forbidden-apis 3.8 may warn or fail to resolve signatures. Speculative/low confidence; not counted against candidates.

points.claim: Only forbiddenApisMain wired, forbiddenApisTest omitted; matches spotbugs convention (line 805 wires only spotbugsMain, line 860 disables spotbugsTest); suggest clarifying comment
points.note: All three cited line numbers verified accurate against the repo; observation real and well-calibrated.

points.claim: ignoreFailures=true appropriate initially, should become false later, add JIRA-referencing comment
points.note: Matches the human reviewer's sole point (ignoreFailures is what keeps the build green).

points.claim: :core IS covered because the plugin scans compiled bytecode (java.srcDirs=[] but joint compilation via scala)
points.note: Verified CORRECT (and directly rebuts v5's fabrication), but it affirms a non-issue rather than finding one, so no credit under the rubric.

points.claim: Trailing newline at EOF removed; restore it
points.note: Verified: base file ends with 0x0a, diff shows '\ No newline at end of file'. Small but real and actionable.

missed: Plugin declared without 'apply false' in the plugins block, inconsistent with spotbugs/scoverage/shadow given the explicit per-subproject apply (own_review #1)
exceeds: forbiddenApisMain/Test wiring asymmetry with verified spotbugs-convention cross-references

points.claim: :core will be SILENTLY EXCLUDED; forbiddenApisMain will have ZERO sources because the plugin resolves sources from the Java source set
points.note: Fabricated mechanism. forbidden-apis is an ASM bytecode scanner; the Gradle plugin checks sourceSet.output.classesDirs, which for :core includes the scala joint-compilation output containing the compiled Java classes. :core is covered. Flagged as headline finding in bold, making it worse.

points.claim: ignoreFailures=true makes the check non-enforcing; add follow-up to enable enforcement
Test sources not checked; only forbiddenApisMain wired vs checkstyle wiring both; add forbiddenApisTest
points.note: Wiring observation verified real, though 'test sources are not checked' is overstated (the plugin hooks forbiddenApis onto check by default) and it misses the spotbugs precedent that v4 caught.

points.claim: Plugin applied to ALL subprojects including organizational-only :connect and :generator
points.note: :connect is indeed an intermediate dir project, but :generator has 48 Java source files and is NOT organizational-only — a false supporting example; the existing subprojects block already applies java-library/checkstyle/spotbugs to the same set, so the concern is moot. Net zero.

points.claim: Only jdk-unsafe signature included; consider adding more later
points.note: Restates the PR description's own stated scope; no new information.

missed: Plugin declared without 'apply false' in the plugins block (own_review #1)

qualitative: This tiny build.gradle PR turned almost entirely on one technical question: does the forbiddenapis Gradle plugin see :core's joint-compiled Java classes? v4 got it exactly right (bytecode scanning over sourceSet output) with verified line-number cross-references to the spotbugs convention, and was the only candidate to catch the real EOF-newline regression. v5 got the same question exactly backwards, fabricating a 'silently excluded, ZERO sources' coverage gap as its bolded headline finding, and padded the review with a false claim that :generator is an organizational-only project. Both candidates covered the human's ignoreFailures point and the forbiddenApisMain/Test wiring asymmetry. Despite seeing the full diff, v5 lands below v4 here because its flagship finding is an absence fabrication and its remaining points are thinner and partly inaccurate.
